translations.titulo: Screening for Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD)
translations.descricao: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) is a progressive chronic respiratory disease, characterized by airflow limitation, being one of the main causes of global morbidity and mortality. Screening aims to identify cases early in at-risk populations through spirometry, reducing complications and mortality. In the Brazilian context, COPD significantly affects the public health system.
translations.sus.indicacao: Screening recommended for individuals with chronic respiratory symptoms, smokers or ex-smokers over 40 years old, and occupational exposure to dusts or fumes.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults ≥40 years with a smoking history ≥20 pack-years, dyspnea or chronic cough, and environmental exposure.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The Global Initiative for Chronic Obstructive Lung Disease (GOLD 2024) and the Brazilian Society of Pulmonology and Tisiology (SBPT 2021) recommend active search (case-finding) in at-risk individuals, not general population screening.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The global prevalence of COPD in adults >40 years is estimated at 12.1%. In Brazil, the prevalence is approximately 13.6% in individuals >40 years.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The global incidence varies from 1.5 to 2.5 cases per 1,000 person-years in at-risk populations. In Brazil, it is estimated at 100,000 new cases annually.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: COPD is the third leading cause of death globally, with 3.23 million deaths in 2019. In Brazil, it accounts for about 5% of deaths from chronic diseases.
